description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a docker:dind service and DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay, defining two stages: version and deploy. In the version stage add a semver job that uses the python:3.7-stretch image, runs a before_script to set up SSH keys, clone the SEMVER_REPO, then executes "pip install semver", "python3 /tmp/semver/gensemver.py > version" and echoes the version, stores the generated version file as an artifact, runs only on master, dev* and rc* branches, and is tagged x86_64. In the deploy stage add six jobs (encode_gate-amd64, encode_gate-arm, encode_gui-amd64, encode_gui-arm, encoder-amd64, loader-amd64) each depending on semver, using the docker:latest image, inheriting a global before_script that exports VERSION from the version file and logs in to the GitLab registry, then runs a script that normalises the branch name (setting CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME to "Stable" if on master), builds the corresponding Docker image with "docker build --pull -t \"$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/<name>:$VERSION-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME-<arch>\" -f Dockerfiles/<name> ." and pushes it with "docker push"; each job runs only on master, dev* and rc* branches, and is tagged either x86_64 or ARM as appropriate.
